title: fixed options not having headless attribute
body:
Fixes errors:

line 398, in __init__
    if headless or options.headless:
                   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'ChromeOptions' object has no attribute 'headless'


line 488, in __init__
    if options.headless:
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'ChromeOptions' object has no attribute 'headless'

step_plan.summary: Fixes crashes when initializing `uc.Chrome(...)` with options objects that do not define a `.headless` attribute by making headless detection attribute-safe and adding a compatibility `.headless` property to the project’s `ChromeOptions`. Ensures headless-specific configuration runs only when headless is actually enabled (via kwarg or headless CLI arguments).

steps.operation: Make headless detection robust by avoiding direct `options.headless` access; compute a single `is_headless` flag from (a) `headless` kwarg, (b) an optional `options.headless` attribute via `getattr`, and (c) presence of `--headless` / `--headless=new` in options arguments; use this flag everywhere in `__init__` where headless behavior is decided.
steps.reason: The reported `AttributeError` originates from unconditional reads of `options.headless`. Some Selenium/UC option objects don’t expose this attribute, but headless still needs to be detected reliably from either the explicit `headless=` parameter or headless CLI args already present in options.
steps.side_effects: May slightly change headless activation precedence (now explicitly defined by the computed `is_headless` rule). Requires verifying that any existing code paths that previously depended on `options.headless` still behave the same. Add/adjust tests or a minimal runtime check to confirm no crash when passing Selenium’s `ChromeOptions`.

steps.operation: Change headless hardening to accept an explicit `is_headless` decision from `__init__` and only apply headless-specific configuration when that flag is true; remove any internal assumptions that `options.headless` exists (e.g., don’t re-check `options.headless` inside this method).
steps.reason: `_configure_headless` is currently gated (directly or indirectly) by `if options.headless:` patterns, which can crash or mis-detect headless. Centralizing headless detection in `__init__` and passing/using a boolean avoids attribute reliance and keeps behavior consistent.
steps.side_effects: If `_configure_headless` previously inferred headless from options state, it will now follow the `__init__` computation. Ensure downstream behavior (flags, user-agent tweaks, window sizing, etc.) still occurs only in headless mode. Might require updating any internal callers if they exist.

steps.operation: Add a compatibility `headless` property (and optional setter) on the project’s `ChromeOptions` that reflects whether headless is enabled based on the presence of `--headless` / `--headless=new` arguments (and, if a setter is provided, adds/removes the appropriate argument rather than setting a bare attribute).
steps.reason: Even with safe `getattr` usage, providing a stable `.headless` API on this project’s own options type reduces future regressions and improves compatibility with code that expects `options.headless` to exist (including older internal logic and user code).
steps.side_effects: Potential behavior change for users who previously set `options.headless = True` expecting it to work (if you implement a setter, it should now work; if not, document the preferred `add_argument('--headless...')` path). Ensure the property implementation matches how arguments are stored (`arguments` list vs other Selenium internals) and doesn’t break serialization/copying of options.
